item.title: Berkshire Hathaway’s Cash Pile Surges to Record $397 Billion
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway Inc.’s cash pile jumped to its highest level ever, reaching $397 billion, in Greg Abel’s first quarter as chief executive officer.

item.title: New Rules for India Shadow Banks May Put Tata Sons IPO on Radar
item.description: A tweak by India’s central bank to the definition of shadow lenders has potentially revived the debate about whether Tata Sons Pvt. — the controller of one of the country’s largest conglomerates — could be forced to list in the near future.

item.title: Reinvention at the Fed and in Las Vegas
item.description: The FOMC held its meeting this week.

item.title: Oil Tanker Pricing Feud Embroils a Centuries-Old London Market
item.description: A legal claim from one of the world’s largest oil traders is throwing a spotlight on an arcane but critical corner of global finance — the multibillion-dollar freight market and the 282-year-old City of London institution that sits at the heart of it.

item.title: OPEC+ Agrees in Principle on Small Output Hike, Reuters Reports
item.description: Seven members of OPEC+ have reached an agreement in principle to raise oil output targets by around 188,000 barrels per day in June, Reuters reported Saturday, citing unnamed sources.

item.title: World’s Largest Container Carrier Plans Route Avoiding Hormuz
item.description: The world’s largest container carrier plans a new service linking Europe with isolated Middle East ports, using trucking across Saudi Arabia and smaller vessels in the Persian Gulf instead of transiting the blocked Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: How Modi Is Ditching Indian Protectionism in Favor of Free Trade
item.description: The prime minister is racking up bilateral deals as he leaves the old ways behind.

item.title: IMF Sees Ivory Coast Growth Easing as Import Costs Drive Deficit
item.description: Ivory Coast’s growth is set to ease as rising import costs widen its external deficit, testing the economy just as the country exits a $4.8 billion International Monetary Fund facility.

item.title: Charting the Global Economy: ECB Is Leaning Toward Rate Hikes
item.description: The European Central Bank is leaning toward lifting interest rates as soon as next month unless there are positive developments on energy prices and ending the Iran war.

item.title: Finland Must Continue to Consolidate Public Finances, Rehn Says
item.description: Finland can no longer avoid or delay dealing with its growing public debt, Bank of Finland Governor Olli Rehn said.

item.title: Rehn Says Fast ECB Action Needed If Second-Round Inflation Seen
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council Member Olli Rehn said it’s “clear” that monetary policy must react fast to any signs of inflation becoming entrenched in the economy through higher wages and prices.

item.title: Chinese Court Rules Firms Can’t Lay Off Workers on AI Grounds
item.description: A Chinese court ruled that companies cannot terminate employees just to replace them with artificial intelligence systems, as authorities juggle the need to stabilize the domestic labor market with a global race to develop AI technologies.

item.title: Inside the Booming Market for Dinosaur Fossils
item.description: Why fossils are selling for millions to wealthy collectors.

item.title: Odd Lots: Inside a Booming Market for Dinosaur Fossils (Podcast)
item.description: Two years ago, Citadel’s Ken Griffin paid almost $45 million or a stegosaurus skeleton, making it the most expensive fossil ever sold at auction. So why are dinosaur bones joining the collections of millionaires instead of museums? How does the private market for fossils actually work? And how similar is it to the market for art and other antiquities? In this episode, we speak with Salomon Aaron, a director at London-based gallery David Aaron, where he is the gallery’s in-house broker for dinosa

item.title: Spirit Shuts Operations After White House Bailout Collapses
item.description: Spirit Aviation Holdings Inc. has started an orderly wind-down of operations, the troubled US discount carrier said in a statement Saturday.

item.title: India-Linked Tanker Laden With Cooking Fuel Attempts Hormuz Exit
item.description: An India-linked supertanker laden with liquefied petroleum gas is attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz, a rare crossing that underscores the country’s struggle to alleviate a historic energy crisis.

item.title: Musk’s Trial Against OpenAI Hits Some Rough Spots in First Week
item.description: Elon Musk set out to tell a jury that his falling out with OpenAI was a simple tale of betrayal.

item.title: Columbia Risks Losing Top Credit Rating as Trump Slams Colleges
item.description: Columbia University’s credit outlook was revised to negative by Moody’s Ratings, which cited rising risks tied to the “federal environment for higher education.”

item.title: EBay Soars on Report That GameStop Is Preparing a Takeover Bid
item.description: EBay Inc. jumped more than 13% in after-hours trading on Friday after the Wall Street Journal reported that video-game retailer GameStop Corp. is preparing a bid for the company.

item.title: Archroma Reaches Final Terms on Much Delayed Loan Extension
item.description: Specialty chemicals producer Archroma sweetened the terms on a junk loan deal to extend around $1 billion of debt, inching closer to completing a transaction that has faced multiple delays over the past month.

item.title: Pershing Square IPO Investors Post Gain After Free Shares
item.description: Investors in Bill Ackman’s new $5 billion closed-end fund ended the week with a small gain overall, once the value of the free shares they received in his asset management company is included.

item.title: LA’s Television City Studio Lot Faces Sale Forced by Lenders
item.description: Lenders are forcing Hollywood landlord Hackman Capital Partners, the largest independent studio and soundstage owner, to relinquish entertainment properties around Los Angeles as values plunge and demand stays soft.

item.title: Japan’s Takaichi to Revamp Indo-Pacific Vision in Vietnam Speech
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi on Saturday will use a speech in Vietnam to lay out her vision for the Indo-Pacific, as she seeks to bolster economic and security ties across the region in a move that could counter an increasingly assertive China.

item.title: AI Chipmaker Cerebras Is Said to Target Up to $4 Billion in IPO
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. is seeking to raise as much as $4 billion in its initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, as demand for the artificial intelligence chipmaker and data center operator’s shares heats up.

item.title: Retailer West Marine Readies Possible Bankruptcy to Close Stores
item.description: Boat retailer West Marine Inc. is laying the groundwork for a potential Chapter 11 bankruptcy to restructure its debt, including leases, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Apple Raises Mac Mini’s Starting Price to $799 After AI Frenzy Drains Supply
item.description: Apple Inc. raised the starting price of its Mac mini desktop to $799 from $599, adjusting to inventory shortages driven by AI demand and the tight supply of processors.

item.title: Disney’s New CEO Is Exploring a ‘Super App’ For Theme Park Tickets, Movies and More
item.description: Walt Disney Co. senior executives are discussing how to unify the company’s disparate mobile apps and turn its streaming service into the first stop for all things Disney, a place where users can book park tickets, buy merchandise, play games and watch movies, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Fast-Money Hedge Fund Trades Roar as Wall Street Defies Gloom
item.description: Wall Street is back in triumphant mode, with stocks at records, risk appetites refreshed — and alternative strategies that run on their own engine winning, too.

item.title: OpenAI’s Revenue Chief Says Enterprise Business ‘Accelerating’
item.description: A recent report raised concerns about OpenAI missing its growth targets.

item.title: The Online Games Where the House Always Wins
item.description: Online casino-style games like Monopoly GO! are huge. Could new scrutiny challenge how they do business?
